title: Curate context, do not stuff it
description: Selected passages are deduplicated and trimmed to fit the window with output headroom, never packed until truncation.
body: Use when assembling retrieved passages into the prompt. Do not concatenate every candidate until the window overflows, and avoid burying the most relevant passage among redundant ones. Actions: deduplicate near-identical passages, order by relevance, trim to a budget that leaves output headroom, and keep citations attached. Validation signals include a test that the assembled context fits the window and that the highest-ranked passages are never the ones truncated away. Risk boundary: stuffing past the window silently drops evidence the answer cites; treat overflow as a failure that breaks grounding.
